name: Wakayama Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the deep waters of the Pacific along the Kii Peninsula coast

description: A white field charged at the centre with a deep-blue emblem stylising the kanji 和 ('harmony') of the prefecture's name.
history: Wakayama's prefectural symbol was adopted on 24 May 1969. The flag, with the navy emblem on a white field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: purity, peace and the harmony evoked by the kanji 和 of the prefecture's name
a stylised abstraction of the kanji 和 (wa, 'harmony') of 和歌山 (Wakayama); the upward-curving form also evokes a flying bird and the prefecture's progress.
